paper_id: Debbech et al. - 2019 - Towards Semantic Interpretation of Goal-Oriented Safety Decisions Based on Foundational Ontology

models.model_label: Semantic interpretation conceptual model of railway safety rules development grounded in UFO (linking Or-BAC and GORE concepts)

evidence_quotes.quote: The aim of this paper is to propose a common vocabulary to ensure the clarity, the completeness and the non-ambiguity of these concepts.
The proposed interpretation provides a common vocabulary to conceptualize the railway domain knowledge and goal-oriented requirements engineering.
The aim of this interpretation is to provide a common vocabulary in real-world semantics in order to have a shared view between different knowledge domains.

goals.justification: The paper explicitly frames the model's purpose as providing a common vocabulary to clarify concepts and support shared understanding/meaning negotiation across safety and design teams, matching the For Understanding signals (clarify, define, conceptualize, shared understanding).

evidence_quotes.quote: This task is challenging in the context of Safety Critical Systems (SCSs) development, since it involves both safety and design teams.
The proposed multi-view conceptual model is shared between different actors.
The aim of this interpretation is to provide a common vocabulary in real-world semantics in order to have a shared view between different knowledge domains.

goals.justification: The model is framed as bridging distinct groups (safety teams, design teams, domain experts) by providing a shared multi-view vocabulary. Per the disambiguation rule, conveying meaning between distinct parties supports For Communicating in addition to Understanding.

evidence_quotes.quote: Moreover, the obtained interpretation may support the tool implementation development of safety rules for the railway domain.

uncertain_goals.reason: The implementation use is framed as a potential future support ('may support', no realized artifact), which the rules treat as hypothetical rather than a present intended purpose.

evidence_quotes.quote: Moreover, the obtained interpretation may support the tool implementation development of safety rules for the railway domain.
improve the safety-decision based on GORE concepts through a reference model.

uncertain_goals.reason: References to improving safety decisions are generic benefits rather than concrete evidence that the model itself guided a specific decision, option analysis, or evaluation of alternatives.
